findings.title: Diagnostic tests use weak any{substring} assertion instead of pinning message and count
findings.why_it_matters: importDerNichtExistiert_meldetDiagnose (line 112) and gleicherFqnInZweiDateien_meldetKollision (line 136) both assert `diagnosen.any { substring in it.meldung }`. This gives false confidence: (a) the test passes if five unrelated diagnostics fire as long as one contains the substring, (b) a future message-text change does not break the test, and (c) neither test guards against duplicate or extra diagnostics. The correct invariant for both paths is exactly one diagnostic with a specific message.
evidence: ModulTest.kt:112-115: assertTrue(ergebnis.diagnosen.any { "mathe.fehlt" in it.meldung }, ...)
ModulTest.kt:136-139: assertTrue(ergebnis.diagnosen.any { "gemein.hilf" in it.meldung }, ...)
Resolver.kt:282-285: actual message is 'Importierter Name X wurde im Projekt nicht gefunden'
Resolver.kt:259-264: actual message is 'Vollqualifizierter Name X wird sowohl in A als auch in B deklariert'
findings.suggested_fix: For each test: assertEquals(1, ergebnis.diagnosen.size, "Erwarte genau eine Diagnose") and then assertTrue("mathe.fehlt" in ergebnis.diagnosen[0].meldung) (or assertEquals on the full message). This pins both count and content.

findings.title: Import-collides-with-local-declaration branch in Resolver.fuehreZusammen is untested
findings.why_it_matters: Resolver.kt lines 274-279 fire when a module both declares a name locally and imports a different FQN under the same short name. The diagnostic message is 'Import X kollidiert mit einer Deklaration im selben Modul'. No test in ModulTest exercises this branch. A regression that removes the check or silently skips the continue would allow ambiguous name resolution without any diagnostic.
evidence: Resolver.kt:274-279: if (kurz in kurzAufFqn) { diagnosen.melde("Import '$kurz' kollidiert mit einer Deklaration im selben Modul", ...) }
ModulTest.kt: no test where a file declares a name locally and also imports a different FQN under the same short name
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test: a file that contains both 'funktion hilf(): Ganzzahl { ... }' and 'importiere fremdes.hilf' should yield exactly one diagnostic containing 'kollidiert'. Verify diagnosen.size == 1 and the message substring.

findings.title: Golden-example test exercises only interpreter; bytecode path for the module example is untested
findings.why_it_matters: beispielModuleLaeuftMitGoldenerAusgabe runs the examples through the interpreter only. The FQN encoding in Bytecodeerzeuger (jvm() '$'-encoding, schreibeKlasse multi-directory output, cross-module method invocation via invokestatic with '$'-encoded names) is never validated against the authoritative golden output. A bytecode regression producing wrong output for the module example would not be caught.
evidence: ModulTest.kt:269: Interpreter(programm, ergebnis.parallelplan!!, { ausgabe.appendLine(it) }).starte()
ModulTest.kt: no kompiliereUndLaufe call using the beispiele/module golden files
The three bytecode tests (lines 178, 208, 226) use synthetic projects, not the golden example
findings.suggested_fix: Extend beispielModuleLaeuftMitGoldenerAusgabe with a second assertion that calls kompiliereUndLaufe(quellen, eintrag=mainDatei.path, hauptklasse="main", startFqn="start") and compares the result to aus.readText().

findings.title: BeispieleTest.goldeneAusgaben uses non-recursive listFiles and silently misses beispiele/module/
findings.why_it_matters: BeispieleTest.goldeneAusgaben calls `beispiele.listFiles { datei -> datei.extension == "edel" }`. Java's File.listFiles() returns only direct children, not descendants. The new beispiele/module/main.edel file is one level deeper and will never be discovered. Any future module example added under a subdirectory will be silently excluded. This also means BeispieleTest provides no backstop if ModulTest is removed.
evidence: BeispieleTest.kt:21: beispiele.listFiles { datei -> datei.extension == "edel" }
Java File.listFiles() returns direct children only (non-recursive)
Filesystem: beispiele/module/main.edel is at depth 2 relative to beispiele/
findings.suggested_fix: Replace listFiles with walkTopDown().filter { it.isFile && it.extension == "edel" }. Note that module examples require multi-file analysis (analysiereProjekt) rather than single-file interpretiere, so the factory loop must detect the presence of paket/importiere directives or the presence of a sibling .aus file at the file's own directory level and dispatch accordingly.

findings.title: Multi-segment package FQN encoding through bytecode backend untested
findings.why_it_matters: jvm() replaces all dots with '$': a function 'f' in package 'a.b' becomes JVM method name 'a$b$f'. All three bytecode tests use single-segment packages (geometrie, zahlen, anwendung). The multi-segment encoding path has never been exercised end-to-end. A bug that drops one segment, double-encodes, or produces an invalid descriptor would only surface at JVM verification time.
evidence: Bytecodeerzeuger.kt:51: private fun jvm(fqn: String): String = fqn.replace('.', '$')
bytecodeUebersetztMehrdateiProjekt: paket geometrie (one segment)
bytecodeUebersetztPaketiertesEinstiegsprogramm: paket anwendung (one segment)
bytecodeUebersetztDatensaetzeAusFremdemPaket: paket zahlen (one segment)
No test uses paket a.b.c or any two-level package
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bytecode test with a two-level package, e.g. 'paket werkzeug.zahlen' containing a datensatz and a function, imported from main.edel. Verify the output value matches expectations.

findings.title: New parser constraint (paket/importiere after declarations raises error) has no test
findings.why_it_matters: Parser.kt lines 104-108 raise QuellFehler when a paket or importiere directive appears after at least one declaration. This replaces the old behavior that silently created ImportDeklaration/PaketDeklaration nodes. The constraint is new and ParserTest has no case for directives at all. A refactor reverting to the old silent behavior would be undetected.
evidence: Parser.kt:104-108: PAKET, IMPORTIERE -> fehler("...-Direktiven muessen am Anfang der Datei stehen, vor allen Deklarationen", ...)
Parser.kt:27-28: duplicate paket directive also raises an error -- also untested
Parser.kt:36-37: importiere without a dot raises an error -- also untested
Parser.kt:39-40: duplicate importiere of same short name raises an error -- also untested
ParserTest.kt: no test references paket or importiere at all
findings.suggested_fix: Add to ParserTest: (1) happy path verifying Programm.paket and Programm.importe are populated; (2) assertFailsWith<QuellFehler> for directive-after-declaration; (3) assertFailsWith<QuellFehler> for duplicate paket; (4) assertFailsWith<QuellFehler> for importiere without a dot.

findings.title: Lambda body referring to an imported function is untested across all backends
findings.why_it_matters: NamensAuflöser.walkeAusdruck walks LambdaAusdruck bodies, setting aufgelöst on any Bezeichner that refers to an imported or module-qualified name. None of the 10 tests include a lambda whose body calls an imported function. The interpreter lookup path (nachschlagen with kurz then fqn) and the bytecode path (jvm(fqn) invokestatic) are therefore untested for lambdas with cross-module references.
evidence: Resolver.kt:419-422: is LambdaAusdruck -> { e.parameter.forEach { walkeTyp(it.typ) }; walkeAusdruck(e.körper) }
Interpreter.kt:983-984: is Bezeichner -> nachschlagen(umgebung, ausdruck.name, ausdruck.aufgelöst)
No ModulTest case: no lambda that closes over or calls an imported function
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test where the imported module exposes a function and the main module passes a lambda that calls it (e.g. sei f = (n: Ganzzahl) -> verdopple(n)). Verify the output.

findings.title: Native binary path passes dotted FQN to native-image but is entirely untested
findings.why_it_matters: befehlBinär now passes ü.hauptklasse (which may be 'anwendung.main') as the main-class argument to native-image. The old code always passed the short file stem. There is no test confirming the argument is correctly formed for multi-package projects. This is acknowledged as untested (GraalVM not available in CI) but the regression risk from the FQN change is unacknowledged in comments.
evidence: Main.kt:385: ü.hauptklasse (may contain dots for packaged entry files)
Old Main.kt: klassenname was always quelldatei.nameWithoutExtension (no dots)
No test file touches befehlBinär or findeNativeImage
findings.suggested_fix: Add a comment in befehlBinär acknowledging the untested dotted-FQN main-class change. Consider a unit test for the main-class-argument construction using ProcessBuilder argument capture, or add a CLI integration test that skips when native-image is absent.

residual_risks: analysiere() now delegates to analysiereProjekt with a synthetic '<inline>' key. If führeZusammen introduces a regression for single-file programs (e.g., wrong ordering or eintrag lookup failure), existing test suites would catch it only incidentally since they test behavior rather than the delegation path itself.

beispielModuleLaeuftMitGoldenerAusgabe uses relative File paths ('beispiele/module/main.edel') that depend on the test CWD being the edel/ subproject root. If both mainDatei.isFile and punktDatei.isFile are false, the assertTrue passes immediately and the actual comparison is never reached -- silent skip rather than test failure.

gleicherFqnInZweiDateien_meldetKollision bypasses sammleProjekt and injects both a.edel and b.edel directly into the quellen Map. This correctly tests Resolver.fuehreZusammen collision detection, but does NOT verify that the CLI's import-closure traversal (sammleProjekt/findeImportdatei) would actually discover both files in a real directory structure.

The Dokumentdienst LSP changes (removal of 'else -> null' and 'else -> {}' branches) are behavioral but have no corresponding LSP test. These are low risk since the branches handled only unexpected sealed-class variants (which Kotlin exhaustive-when already covers at compile time), but the absence of LSP tests means no regression safety net exists.
testing_gaps: No test for import-collides-with-local-declaration (Resolver.kt:274): a file that both declares and imports under the same short name.
No test for multi-segment package (a.b.c) end-to-end through bytecode or interpreter.
No bytecode test using the authoritative golden beispiele/module/ example.
No parser tests for any of the new directive-parsing branches: paket, importiere, duplicate paket, importiere-without-dot, duplicate importiere short name, directive-after-declaration.
No test for lambda body calling an imported function in any backend.
No test for datensatz whose field type is an imported datensatz from another module (cross-module field-type dependency distinct from function-parameter position).
No test for AufzählungDeklaration across a module boundary (enum type imported and used in another module).
BeispieleTest.goldeneAusgaben does not recurse into subdirectories; new module examples added under beispiele/ subdirectories will be silently excluded.
Native binary path (befehlBinär with dotted hauptklasse argument to native-image) is entirely untested.
